examples.title: Exception Class Hierarchy Example
examples.description: Demonstrates creating custom exception hierarchy with proper exception handling
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates proper exception class hierarchy design. CustomException is the base, with DatabaseException and NetworkException as derived classes. The catch blocks are ordered from most derived to base, ensuring specific exceptions are caught first.
examples.timeComplexity: Depends on exception type and handling complexity

examples.title: Stack Unwinding and Constructor Exceptions
examples.description: Shows how stack unwinding works when constructors throw exceptions
examples.explanation: This demonstrates that when a constructor throws, already-constructed member objects (like Resource) have their destructors called during stack unwinding. This ensures proper cleanup even when construction fails.
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for exception handling, O(n) for stack unwinding where n is call stack depth

examples.title: Rethrowing Exceptions
examples.description: Demonstrates exception rethrowing for partial handling
examples.explanation: Rethrowing allows a catch block to perform partial handling (like logging) and then pass the exception to outer handlers. The throw; statement without an operand rethrows the current exception object.
examples.timeComplexity: O(n) where n is the number of times exception is rethrown and caught

examples.title: Exception Safety Guarantees
examples.description: Demonstrates different levels of exception safety
examples.explanation: This code demonstrates all three exception safety guarantees: nothrow (createInt never throws), strong guarantee (all-or-nothing with copy-swap), and basic guarantee (no resource leaks).
